title: The VAE Decoder Component
concept: After latent denoising finishes, a VAE decoder (callback to Units 305-314) converts the final latent code into a full-resolution pixel image.

exercises.prompt: When you finish sketching a picture in tiny code form on a computer, what still needs to happen before you can print it out full-size?

options.text: The tiny code needs to be expanded back into a full, detailed picture
The tiny code becomes the final picture automatically, no changes needed
The tiny code needs to be deleted first

exercises.explain: A compressed 'code' version of an image isn't the final picture yet — something has to unpack it into full detail!

cards.title: The Final Reveal
cards.body: The denoiser just spent many steps cleaning up a fuzzy latent code until it's nice and sharp. But wait — that latent code is still tiny and compressed, not a real picture yet! Someone has to turn it into the full image you actually see. That someone is the VAE decoder.

cards.title: What Is the VAE Decoder?
cards.body: The VAE decoder is the final piece of the pipeline. Once denoising is done, it takes the clean latent code and expands it back into a full-resolution pixel image — the picture you can actually open and view.

cards.title: Unfolding a Packed Box
cards.body: Think of the latent code like a suitcase packed super tight for a trip. The VAE decoder is like unpacking that suitcase at home — unfolding every shirt and item back to its full size so you can actually wear it. The suitcase was convenient to carry, but you need to unpack it to use what's inside.

cards.title: From Code to Cat Photo
cards.body: The denoiser finishes with a clean latent code that secretly represents 'a fluffy orange cat.' The VAE decoder takes that small code and expands it into a full 512x512 pixel image showing fur texture, whiskers, and colors — the final picture you download.

cards.title: Myth: The Denoiser Makes the Final Picture
cards.body: Myth: Once denoising stops, you already have the finished image. Truth: Denoising only cleans up the latent code — it's still compressed. The VAE decoder is the one that actually builds the full-size pixel image you see on screen.

cards.title: Latent Code vs Final Image
cards.body: These two things look nothing alike, but one becomes the other thanks to the VAE decoder.

left.text: Small, compressed grid of numbers — not viewable as a picture

right.text: Full-resolution pixel picture, ready to view or share

exercises.prompt: What does the VAE decoder do at the end of the pipeline?

options.text: It converts the final latent code into a full-resolution pixel image
It adds noise to the latent code
It deletes the latent code entirely

exercises.explain: The VAE decoder's job is to expand the compressed latent code into the full, viewable pixel image.

exercises.prompt: True or False: After denoising finishes, the latent code is already a viewable picture with no further steps needed.
exercises.explain: False — the latent code still needs the VAE decoder to convert it into a full pixel image.

exercises.sentence: The VAE decoder converts the final latent code into a full-resolution ___ image.
exercises.explain: The VAE decoder turns the compact latent code into a full-resolution pixel image you can actually see.

exercises.prompt: Which of these best describes the relationship between the latent code and the decoded image?

options.text: The latent code is a compressed version that the decoder expands into the full image
The latent code and the decoded image are exactly the same thing
The decoded image is made without ever using the latent code

exercises.explain: The latent code is compact and compressed; the VAE decoder expands it into the full pixel image.

exercises.prompt: A student says: 'Once the denoising loop stops, the image generation is basically finished — the latent code IS the picture.' What's wrong with this statement?

options.text: Nothing — the latent code is already the final picture
The latent code is still compressed data, not a pixel image — the VAE decoder must convert it into the full-resolution picture first
The denoiser and the decoder are the same component
The text prompt becomes the picture, not the latent code

exercises.explain: Denoising only cleans up the latent code. The VAE decoder is a separate, essential step that expands that compact code into the full-resolution image you can actually see.
